title: Recesiune politică severă
summary: Cristian Preda argues that Romania's most severe recession is political, not economic. The governing coalition is paralyzed by internal disputes, delayed appointments, and tax increases, while President Nicușor Dan risks repeating Klaus Iohannis's pattern of indolence and disconnection from voters.
talking_points: The economic recession debate is a distraction from the deeper political recession undermining Romanian democracy
The four-party coalition (PSD, PNL, USR, UDMR) has only managed consensus on raising taxes, while failing on reforms and key appointments
PM Bolojan has publicly expressed frustration with his mandate, while PSD figures like Grindeanu criticize their own government more harshly than the opposition
President Nicușor Dan's indolence and denial of coalition dysfunction echoes the detachment that characterized Klaus Iohannis's presidency
In functional democracies, coalition agreements are detailed and binding — Romania's coalition operates in the opposite manner
author: Cristian Preda
